entries.definition:
جَمِيل jamīl [quasi-act. part.] 1 gracious (15:85) إِنَّ السَّاعَةَ لَآتِيَةٌ فَاصْفَحِ الصَّفْحَ الْجَمِيلَ the Hour is certainly coming, so, forgive with gracious forgiveness 2 generous, amicable (33:28) فَتَعَالَيْنَ أُمَتِّعْكُنَّ وَأُسَرِّحْكُنَّ سَرَاحًا جَمِيلًا then come, I will make provision for you and release you [from the bondage of marriage] with amicable release 3 gracious, charitable, free of rancour, free of bitterness (73:10) وَاصْبِرْ عَلَى مَا يَقُولُونَ وَاهْجُرْهُمْ هَجْرًا جَمِيلًا patiently endure what they say, and part with them with a gracious parting 4 comely, goodly, worthy, fitting (12:83) فَصَبْرٌ جَمِيلٌ عَسَى اللَّهُ أَنْ يَأْتِيَنِي بِهِمْ جَمِيعًا so comely patience [is most fitting for me]: may God bring all of them back to me.
جِمَالَتٌ jimālatun [coll. n.; n. for the species; pl. of pl. n. جِمَال jimāl] (variously interpreted as) camels; thick ropes; copper (77:33) كَأَنَّهُ جِمَالَتٌ صُفْرٌ and as bright as yellow copper (or, like giant fiery ropes, like yellow camels).

جَمَل jamal [n.] camel; thick rope (7:40) وَلَا يَدْخُلُونَ الْجَنَّةَ حَتَّى يَلِجَ الْجَمَلُ فِي سَمِّ الْخِيَاطِ and they will not enter the Garden until a thick rope passes through the eye of a needle.

جُمْلَةٌ jumlatun [n., used adverbially] whole, in totality, all at once (25:32) لَوْلَا نُزِّلَ عَلَيْهِ الْقُرْآنُ جُمْلَةً وَاحِدَةً the unbelievers said, ‘If only the Qurʾan was sent down to him in one body!’
entries.definition_english:
جَمِيل jamīl [quasi-act. part.] 1 gracious (15:85) إِنَّ السَّاعَةَ لَآتِيَةٌ فَاصْفَحِ الصَّفْحَ الْجَمِيلَ the Hour is certainly coming, so, forgive with gracious forgiveness 2 generous, amicable (33:28) فَتَعَالَيْنَ أُمَتِّعْكُنَّ وَأُسَرِّحْكُنَّ سَرَاحًا جَمِيلًا then come, I will make provision for you and release you [from the bondage of marriage] with amicable release 3 gracious, charitable, free of rancour, free of bitterness (73:10) وَاصْبِرْ عَلَى مَا يَقُولُونَ وَاهْجُرْهُمْ هَجْرًا جَمِيلًا patiently endure what they say, and part with them with a gracious parting 4 comely, goodly, worthy, fitting (12:83) فَصَبْرٌ جَمِيلٌ عَسَى اللَّهُ أَنْ يَأْتِيَنِي بِهِمْ جَمِيعًا so comely patience [is most fitting for me]: may God bring all of them back to me.
جِمَالَتٌ jimālatun [coll. n.; n. for the species; pl. of pl. n. جِمَال jimāl] (variously interpreted as) camels; thick ropes; copper (77:33) كَأَنَّهُ جِمَالَتٌ صُفْرٌ and as bright as yellow copper (or, like giant fiery ropes, like yellow camels).

جَمَل jamal [n.] camel; thick rope (7:40) وَلَا يَدْخُلُونَ الْجَنَّةَ حَتَّى يَلِجَ الْجَمَلُ فِي سَمِّ الْخِيَاطِ and they will not enter the Garden until a thick rope passes through the eye of a needle.

جُمْلَةٌ jumlatun [n., used adverbially] whole, in totality, all at once (25:32) لَوْلَا نُزِّلَ عَلَيْهِ الْقُرْآنُ جُمْلَةً وَاحِدَةً the unbelievers said, ‘If only the Qurʾan was sent down to him in one body!’

entries.definition:
j–m–m large group of people, multitude, to abound, to be plentiful; forelock; to relax. Of this root, جَمّ jamm occurs once in the Qurʾan.
جَمّ jamm [quasi-act. part., used adjectivally] very much, abounding, dearly (89:20) وَتُحِبُّونَ الْمَالَ حُبًّا جَمًّا and you love wealth with excessive love.
entries.definition_english:
j–m–m large group of people, multitude, to abound, to be plentiful; forelock; to relax. Of this root, جَمّ jamm occurs once in the Qurʾan.
جَمّ jamm [quasi-act. part., used adjectivally] very much, abounding, dearly (89:20) وَتُحِبُّونَ الْمَالَ حُبًّا جَمًّا and you love wealth with excessive love.
